title: Level 10.3
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0,0,0,255); --theme-link-color: #747474; }} {{Blurbackground|file=Bern.png}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Azeret+Mono:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Syne+Mono&display=swap');}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 10.3: "The Barns"}}
{{Alt Class|ent=3|env=1|ext=4|no=10.3}}{{Underline|ffffff}} ==
Description
 == {{Blurimage|file=The_Barn.webp|caption=Photo captured of the yard of the Home Barn.}} '''Level 10.3''', otherwise called "'''The Barns'''," is a sublevel between [[Level 10]] and [[Level 11]]. It appears as an undivided maize farm that stretches seemingly infinitely in every direction with small, sparsely placed barns in the middle of the field. Level 10.3's average climate consists of constant puffy overcast clouds with light showers at random unpredictable intervals. The most striking feature of Level 10.3's weather is the lack of daylight at any point. Despite this, several signs indicate that there is, in fact, a day/night cycle. The cycle has two phases, the Twilight and the Abyss, which shift every 14 hours. The Twilight's light is roughly equivalent to the amount of light at midnight in [[The Frontrooms]] (with the help of the small moon in the sky), while the Abyss has virtually no light whatsoever besides the porch lights on every barn. Level 10.3 is one of the most prominent examples of levels affected negatively by [[Phenomenon 15|'''Phenomenon 15''']], resulting in no wanderers being able to communicate with one another. This makes communication between wanderers extremely difficult and especially makes colonies and outposts impossible to accomplish. Every wanderer starts out in what is called the '''Home Barn'''''.'' This barn is fully furnished and has working power, with lights, a gas oven, a freezer, and even a vintage television set. Most wanderers who enter stay in the Home Barn for extended periods of time because any dangers in the level stay out of the light and rarely enter the barns that seem to be meant for people to stay in. The Home Barn also has a backyard separated by a tall fence and taller wheat, as opposed to the corn found everywhere else in the level. The backyard sports a 'ball pit' with a kiddy slide leading into it, as well as a model tractor, several toys, and cow cutouts lining the fence. Outside of the Home Barn, the topography is quite bumpy, having small hills and valleys that eventually even out. The entire land is covered in maize with no discernible paths of any kind to assist with navigation. The corn is edible and is the main food source for those living in the level since not many other objects actually can spawn here. The soil is an extremely dark brown in color and is incredibly spongy and nutrient-filled. Other than the corn, some foreign plants and structures can be found lying in the plains. Witnesses say they have seen trees on top of the highest hills (dead and alive) and lengthy fence gates in the largest valleys. The purpose and origin of these things are all unknown, but they are theorized to support the ecosystem of organisms and microbial life (even if we don't understand how). The fields are where all of the entities on the level dwell, so wanderers who enter are urged to bring a light source, since during the Abyss, entities are visually undetectable. ==
Outer Barns
 == The '''Outer Barns''' refer to any large structures that are present on Level 10.3 other than the Home Barn. These buildings are unevenly spread across the entire level, with some directly next to each other, while others can be up to a kilometer away from any other structures, but their distance from other Outer Barns seems to have no effect as to what is inside each one. Every wanderer sees a different layout of Outer Barns when they enter, and those who have entered the level more than once have stated that they have moved or vanished. The following is a description of every confirmed type of Outer Barn. ===
Forsaken Barn
 === {{Blurimage|file=SpawnismSMH.png|caption=An image of the interior of a Forsaken Barn.}} '''Forsaken Barns''' are the most common and most useful barns for survival. These Outer Barns, not unlike the Home Barn, seem to be modeled for human housing; however, Forsaken Barns, as their name suggests, are abandoned and messy. One can expect to find misplaced furniture, broken household objects, and a lack of an energy supply, with the only exception being the porch lights. Most Forsaken Barns have a door to what would be the basement, but this door is always locked and is unable to be broken down, although a key can manifest somewhere within the Forsaken Barn or in the Home barn, which can be used to open the door and exit the level. Forsaken Barns are notably far colder than the level's already chilly climate, and they smell of "cinnamon and rust," according to several eyewitness testimonies of wandering merchants. The reason these Outer Barns are so helpful is because they are prone to manifesting objects that come of great use, such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]]. However, Forsaken Barns are often occupied by entities, so caution is advised when scavenging. ===
Stables
 === '''Stables''' are statistically the rarest of all of the Outer Barns, and they are also the longest, measuring at about 310 meters at maximum and 120 meters minimum. They have no doors to get inside; instead, the two smallest sides of the structure are open, consistently making it a home to entities due to ease of entry. The ground under a Stable is different from the rest of the terrain of the level, being tan in color, dusty, and primarily made of clay with gravel sprinkled across the top. Objects seldom appear within the Stables, but when they do, they will often generate in a stable. Stable doors, while not that troublesome, lock immediately upon being closed and cannot be opened from the inside, so wanderers will have to crawl underneath if they are trapped inside. ===
Coops
 === {{Blurimage|file=CuriosityKilledWho.png|caption=Only recovered image of a Coop. Photographer presumed deceased.}}'''Coops''' are the tallest Outer Barn, and often the largest. These structures are made of tin and can get to be around four stories tall. Unlike the other Outer Barns, a Coop's porch light is always on no matter what phase of the day/night cycle the level is in. The interior of the Coops consists of hundreds of empty cages stacked on top of each other, a carpet of hay, and ladders to get to the other floors. Each floor has a hole in the center, allowing a direct view of the ceiling from the bottom floor. Every floor has a similar layout of cages, hay strands, and empty glass bottles, except the top floor, which has several shelves with objects on them. These objects, while no examples were ever mentioned in any interviews, were apparently higher in value than any other object source in the level. Unfortunately, the structural stability of Coops are unstable. The building could cave in on itself at any moment, resulting in certain death. We advise everyone not to enter a Coop unless they are sure that it is stable. ==
Entities
 == Level 10.3 has a small variety of native entities that roam the fields and the Outer Barns. The level has entities that are both hostile and docile.
|-|Scary Crows= {{Blurimage|file=ScaryCrow.png|caption=An image of a Scary Crow.}}{{Blurimage|file=Scare_Crow.jpg|caption=Ditto, but brightened.}} '''Scary Crows''' are the most populous entity on Level 10.3, known for keeping watch over the fields and being a thorn in wanderers' sides. These entities measure at about 3 meters tall (8-10 feet) and weigh in at a mere 22 kilograms (50 pounds). Scary Crows are made out of long, straight twigs, hay, stuffing, and cloth. Their bodies are filled with compacted hay and cotton, with their 'skeleton' being formed out of the twigs and the cloth to keep the stuffing in place. The majority of their height comes from their legs, as their head and body make less than a third of their total height. Their cloth is tied down at the neck and waist, and it drapes below the field to protect their legs. Scary Crows are very aggressive towards wanderers and will pursue them until either they have killed the wanderer, they lose track of the wanderer, or the wanderer kills them. Scary Crows prioritize stealth over brute strength and will attempt to sneak up on wanderers before attacking. The most common way Scary Crows try to sneak up on prey is by trying not to move while the victim is looking at them. When the victim is looking away, they quickly and quietly try to close the distance between them. When the Scary Crow is close enough, it attacks. Scary Crow attacks are always fatal, but there are many ways to escape a Scary Crow before it gets the chance to do so. Scary Crows are able to sense the smallest vibrations of nearby corn, but they don't have that same tracking ability inside of the Outer or Home Barns. Most of the time, they will give up as soon as you enter one and return to their original perch, but if they continue the pursuit, you can hide from them quite easily. They also aren't too difficult to incapacitate or kill either. If a wanderer manages to knock them off their feet, it takes them a long time to get back up, which allows the victim to tear the cloth off, which immediately kills them. This can be done while they are standing as well, but it is extremely risky, since despite having a thin and vulnerable body, they are incredibly strong. |-|Trespassers= {{Blurimage|file=Trespassers.jpg|caption=Trespasser in the recovered image of a Coop zoomed in.}} '''Trespassers''', while not as common as Scary Crows, are still prevalent threats in Level 10.3. These entities are most commonly found near, or inside of, the Outer Barns, but can also be found wandering through the fields towards the end of the Twilight phase. They appear as thin, lanky, armless, long-necked humanoids, measuring at ~5 meters (16 feet) tall and weighing ~162 kilograms (357 pounds). They are a dark brown in color but have the ability to slightly change their skin color to blend in with their surroundings. Their body structure is remarkably similar to human beings, but many connections have been made to [[Entity 6|Dullers]] and [[Entity 112|Starers]], who also happen to inhabit the level. Trespassers' feet have only two toes, which end in points rather than toenails, and they have overly large heels. These biological features make the entity very good at balancing despite the lack of arms. Trespassers are known for utilizing Outer Barns, specifically Forsaken Barns as their personal den and using them as their defense from anything they consider a threat, including wanderers. If caught far outside of their Outer Barn, they rarely attack if they see a wanderer and instead will sprint directly towards their den and hide away until they think the coast is clear. That is, if the entity even sees the wanderer in the first place. Trespassers have very poor vision and take a couple of seconds to comprehend what they think they saw, meaning if a wanderer gets out of sight as soon as the entity looks in their direction, even if the entity saw them, they will pay no attention to the wanderer. After several tests, it takes about two seconds for the entity to perceive a threat and react accordingly. This is especially helpful if someone intends to invade their den to either scavenge for items or exit the level. Trespassers aren't very intelligent or persistent either, resulting in an entity that is quite easy to hide from. If a trespasser does fully comprehend an intruder in their home, they will proceed to use every part of their body to bash, batter, and beat the wanderer to death, often attacking the corpse long after death. It is imperative that you be defensive and aware in the presence of a Trespasser. |-|Pests= {{Blurimage|file=Bird_Beaks.png|caption=An image of a Pest shrouded in corn. Note that the path was made by the photographer and is not natural.}} {{Blurimage|file=PeskyBee.jpg|caption=Depiction of a Pest.}} '''Pests''' are the most common entity found on Level 10.3, and also are the least dangerous. As their name suggests, Pests are more annoying rather than dangerous, but have also been described as "unintentionally terrifying." These creatures resemble large, grotesque pigeons or turkeys and get to be about one meter tall (three feet) with two meter wingspan. Instead of feathers, Pests have short, gray fur with a colorful underbelly. They have four, red, bird-like legs and long tail feathers. Most notably, they have deep red, star-shaped heads with no beak or mouth, just their two humanoid eyes in the center. Pests live in packs of 6-12, with an average of two young at a time. They are notorious with their fascination with humans, constantly following wanderers around and flying near them. This behavior seems to have no rhyme or reason, but for this reason, farmers in the level must be wary. Many people who live in Level 10.3 require their nearby fields to survive, and Pests make that significantly more difficult if they aren't chased away. Pests don't eat the corn, instead, similarly to plants, suck up a special nutrient from the soil through their needle-like talons. This doesn't kill the corn, but it does make the corn taste bitter and inedible. A way to avoid Pests is by simply yelling. They seem to be extremely sensitive to loud noises, and one scream can make an entire pack of them fly away in an instant.
==
Entrances & Exits
 == ===
Entrances
=== *Noclipping or falling asleep in the bushes of [[Level 10]] will lead here. *Noclipping into a fence of [[Level 204]] most often leads to Level 10, but can lead here as well. ===
Exits
=== *Entering a Coop after surviving in the Level for 1-3 months and noclipping through the floor after falling through the center will lead to [[Level 98]]. *Entering a Forsaken Barn, using the basement key and going in will seamlessly transition into [[The Basement]] or [[Level 1]] (this particular exit was discovered secondly and is mainly used by merchants).
